sql_code: -- Seven clouds, one schema. Every cloud has a table <cloud>_all with every
-- instance type, plus curated views like <cloud> (comparable current-gen VMs).
-- The cloudspecs* views UNION all clouds and add a 'cloud' column (see Help).
max(net_gbitps)                          AS max_net_gbitps -- NULL: not published
-- Each cloud also has per-cloud tables with an identical schema: aws_all,
-- aws (comparable slice), aws_family, aws_accel, aws_shared - and likewise
-- for gcp, azure, oracle, ovh, hetzner, stackit. What did AWS launch lately?
SELECT family, instance, category, processor_model, arch,
vcpus, ram_gib, round(price_hour, 3) AS usd_hour, release_year
FROM aws_family -- one representative (largest) instance per family

description: Find a VM: Cheapest 16 vCPUs + 64 GiB
sql_code: -- The cloudspecs view is the comparable slice of all seven clouds:
SELECT cloud, instance, arch, processor_model, price_hour,
WHERE vcpus = 16 AND ram_gib = 64 AND arch = 'x86_64'
-- keep the cheapest per cloud (drop this line to see all 74 candidates):
QUALIFY row_number() OVER (PARTITION BY cloud ORDER BY price_hour) = 1
-- Each row feeds one point of the boxplot on the right.
SELECT cloud, instance, price_hour / vcpus AS usd_per_vcpu_hour
r_code: to_svg <- svgstring(width = output.width.inch, height = output.height.inch, scaling = 1)
### the SQL result is bound to the data frame 'df';
### palette.cloud is a predefined cloud -> color mapping
output <- ggplot(df, aes(x = reorder(cloud, usd_per_vcpu_hour, median),
labs(x = NULL, y = 'USD per vCPU-hour (log scale)',
plot(output); dev.off(); to_svg() # render to the html page

sql_code: -- Which vCPU/RAM combinations does each cloud actually sell?
SELECT cloud, vcpus, ram_gib, count(*) AS n
r_code: to_svg <- svgstring(width = output.width.inch, height = output.height.inch, scaling = 1)
# one panel per cloud; dot size = number of instance types with that shape
output <- ggplot(df, aes(x = vcpus, y = ram_gib, size = n, color = cloud)) +
title = 'The VM shape grid of each cloud')

sql_code: -- How many terabytes can one dollar move? Cross-cloud version of Fig. 4 in
-- the Cloudspecs paper: the best deal available in each cloud over time.
FROM cloudspecs_family -- one representative instance per family
WHERE net_gbitps IS NOT NULL -- Hetzner and STACKIT publish no bandwidth
AND price_hour > 0 AND release_year IS NOT NULL
SELECT cloud, year, -- running maximum: the best deal up to that year
max(tb_per_usd) OVER (PARTITION BY cloud ORDER BY year) AS best_tb_per_usd
r_code: to_svg <- svgstring(width = output.width.inch, height = output.height.inch, scaling = 1)
output <- ggplot(df, aes(x = year, y = best_tb_per_usd, color = cloud)) +
labs(x = 'Release year', y = 'Best network transfer (TB/$, log scale)',
title = 'Network bandwidth per dollar keeps improving - unlike CPU and DRAM')

sql_code: -- GPU/accelerator instances live in the *_accel views.
WHERE model IN (SELECT model FROM gpu GROUP BY ALL HAVING count(*) >= 3)
r_code: to_svg <- svgstring(width = output.width.inch, height = output.height.inch, scaling = 1)
labs(x = 'USD per GPU-hour (log scale, cheapest instance)', y = NULL,
title = 'The same GPU can cost 4x more, depending on the cloud')

description: Zoom In: Price Scaling within AWS Families
sql_code: -- Single-cloud views: aws, gcp, azure, oracle, ovh, hetzner, stackit
-- (identical schema everywhere - swap the table name below and re-run).
r_code: to_svg <- svgstring(width = output.width.inch, height = output.height.inch, scaling = 1)
# straight parallel lines on a log-log plot: within a family, doubling
# vCPUs doubles the price, so per-core metrics are size-independent
output <- ggplot(df, aes(x = vcpus, y = price_hour, color = family)) +
title = 'Price scales linearly with instance size')

description: Case Study: KV-Store Node, Any Cloud
sql_code: -- A key-value store node reads 4 KiB values from local NVMe and ships them
-- over the network; its throughput is the smaller of the two bandwidths.
-- Which instance - in any cloud - offers the best capacity and throughput per $?
SELECT cloud, family, instance, price_hour, storage_gb, net_gbitps,
WHERE storage_is_nvme AND storage_read_iops > 0 AND net_gbitps > 0
-- clouds that do not publish NVMe IOPS drop out here
r_code: to_svg <- svgstring(width = output.width.inch, height = output.height.inch, scaling = 1)
# top-right = pareto-optimal; check_overlap hides colliding labels
output <- ggplot(df, aes(x = gb_hours_per_usd, y = eff_gbit_per_usd,
geom_text(check_overlap = TRUE, nudge_y = .25, size = 4, show.legend = FALSE) +
title = 'KV-store node: capacity/$ vs. effective bandwidth/$')
